faqs.a: It's when a person unconsciously redirects feelings, expectations, or patterns from an important past relationship, often a parent or early caregiver, onto their therapist. The person usually doesn't notice it at first.

faqs.q: Is transference a sign that something has gone wrong?
faqs.a: No. It's a normal part of treatment and isn't done on purpose. A skilled therapist pays attention to it because it offers a live look at how the person connects with others.

faqs.q: Does transference only mean romantic feelings?
faqs.a: No. Transference can be warm, hostile, dependent, competitive, or any mix of those. There's also countertransference, which is the therapist's own emotional reactions to the client.
